insights.insight: Ad tech charges $5–$150 CPA for modeled attribution; verified receipt signals settle at roughly $0.10 each — a 50–200x cost difference that isn't a negotiating point, it's a structural break in how purchase confirmation is priced.

With Chrome completing cookie deprecation and fingerprinting under regulatory pressure, the industry's fallback is first-party data that stops at each platform's edge — verified receipt signals are the only cross-ecosystem purchase confirmation that doesn't require identity attachment.

insights.topic: Inbox as the only cross-platform purchase record
insights.insight: Amazon sees Amazon, Walmart sees Walmart — no platform sees the full consumer purchase picture except the email inbox, which receives receipts from every vendor; the protocol converts that universal record into a queryable, identity-free dataset.

insights.topic: Dataset accumulation as the real moat
insights.insight: DKIM verification is two-decade-old technology, so the competitive barrier isn't the pipeline — it's the 4,001 verified receipts already accumulated; a new entrant starts at zero, and every receipt added compounds the signal advantage.

Five pending vendor requests are developer infrastructure platforms (Vercel, Render, DigitalOcean, Hetzner), which would add verified SaaS spend alongside physical retail — the first cross-domain expansion of the verified purchase record.

The network now spans 27 CBSAs including non-metro rural markets, growing through social graph propagation rather than paid acquisition — the dataset is trending toward national household spend representation, not just urban consumer behavior.
